analysis.object: T913: Boolean Circuits Through EML depth analysis

boolean_function.description: Boolean function f:{0,1}^n -> {0,1}. Input and output are EML-0 (discrete bits).

circuit_depth.description: Circuit depth = number of gate layers. Depth is a COUNT (EML-0).

circuit_size.description: Circuit size = number of gates. Size is a COUNT. But SIZE as a function of input length n: s(n) = resource measure = EML-2 (logarithmic in n).
circuit_size.reason: Size as function of n: EML-2

polynomial_circuits.description: P = polynomial size circuits (per input length). Polynomial = EML-2. P/poly is circuits of polynomial size.

exponential_circuits.description: NP-complete functions (under P≠NP) require exponential circuits. Exponential > polynomial = jumps from EML-2 toward EML-inf.

shannon_lower_bound.description: Shannon 1949: most Boolean functions require exp-size circuits. These are EML-inf in circuit complexity. Explicit functions at EML-inf = hard problem.
shannon_lower_bound.reason: Shannon: most functions are EML-inf circuit

t913_theorem.description: T913: Boolean circuit size is EML-2 (polynomial). NP-complete under P≠NP requires EML-inf circuit size (exponential). The jump from polynomial (EML-2) to exponential (EML-inf) is the EML-4 gap. T913.
t913_theorem.reason: Circuit size: EML-2 poly vs EML-inf exp
